checks.finding: REQ-001..REQ-005 all resolve to observable checks: REQ-001 to verbatim source-list/checklist text (AC-001/002/004) and a table-column diff (AC-003); REQ-002 to fixture-driven real-script assertions on the fetch-failure/diff-detected/no-diff/dedup/self-registration/issue-body-allowlist branches (AC-005..011, AC-020, AC-021, all mapped to TEST-005..011/020/021); REQ-003 to a hash comparison (AC-013) and existing-suite green re-run (AC-014); REQ-004 to a document-prose-scan (AC-015) plus file existence/non-existence (AC-016) plus a named review target against issue #149's schema description (AC-017); REQ-005 to CHANGELOG-entry and validate-repository checks (AC-018/019). No requirement rests on unmeasurable intent.

Each Goal (REQ-001..REQ-005) has AC coverage: REQ-001->AC-001..004; REQ-002->AC-005..011,020,021; REQ-003->AC-012..014; REQ-004->AC-015..017; REQ-005->AC-018..019. acceptance-tests.md's 21-row table confirms a clean 1:1 AC-TEST pairing with no gaps or renumbering versus round 1.

All 21 ACs specify externally observable artifacts (file text/columns/permission blocks), fixture-driven script behavior (stubbed gh invocation counts/arguments, exit codes), or named review targets (AC-008 recorded manual verification, AC-017 review against issue #149's schema). New AC-020 specifies a concrete zero-invocation assertion; new AC-021 specifies a concrete positive(allowlisted token present)/negative(adversarial substring absent) pair -- neither is vague intent.

Non-goals explicitly excludes C1 implementation, direct contracts/ writes by check-model-freshness.sh, a precise official-page parser, a .ps1 twin of the shell script itself, extending existing v1/v2 parity suites, a new WFI template artifact, and self-improvement.yml modification -- each with a citation to the deciding section (design.md Design Decisions/API-Contract Plan, OQ-005).

Security Boundaries table (B1..B4) covers external-fetch trust (now explicitly extended to issue bodies per AC-021), registry write boundary, protected-file boundary (with the round-2-resolved pre-merge-commit timing), and fixture isolation. Migration constraint: v1 registry stays byte-for-byte frozen (AC-013). Approval boundary: human-copy application is now pinned to 'commit on the feature PR branch before merge' consistently across AC-011, Roles and Permissions, Main Workflows T-003, and Edge Cases, with the pre-application red CI stated as the designed fail-closed behavior and no fallback.

Risks section names High (external Pillar-C dependency), Medium (fetch-diff heuristic imprecision), Medium (protected-file miss), and Low (two-workflow maintenance) risks, each with a concrete validation surface: External Blocker re-verification at task start (Main Workflows point 4), conservative-heuristic/human-triage design (Non-goals), AC-011's explicit citation of guard_invariants.py:4, and design.md's separation rationale. Not SKIPped because high-risk claims and their validation surfaces are both present.
